page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.xlarge is a memory-optimized eighth-generation server instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 4 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

This server type is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per virtual CPU. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance.

Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. The lack of local storage and the moderate baseline network bandwidth represent key architectural tradeoffs, making the instance dependent on external network performance for disk I/O. However, the high memory density relative to the vCPU count provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute.

The r8ib.xlarge is designed for workloads requiring significant memory capacity relative to compute power. Suitable use cases include medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, real-time analytics, and enterprise applications that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not require high compute density. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. These specifications make the r8ib.xlarge well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring a substantial memory footprint without the overhead of excess CPU cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed, operating on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 Intel vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and dedicated Intel processors for memory-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor for dedicated resource allocation
Baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps with no local storage included
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
